title: Add namespace-scoped RBAC and --namespace flag for multi-tenant
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#123](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/123)

1. Add `--namespace` string flag to `internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go` alongside existing flags (lines 66-84); when set, configure `cache.Options{DefaultNamespaces: map[string]cache.Config{ns: {}}}` at line 91.
2. Add `rbac.namespaceScoped: false` toggle to `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml`.
3. Create `templates/role.yaml` (new) mirroring the 12 apiGroup rule blocks from `clusterrole.yaml`, guarded by `{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}`.
4. Create `templates/rolebinding.yaml` (new) binding ServiceAccount to Role, same conditional.
5. Wrap existing `templates/clusterrole.yaml` and `templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml` in `{{- if not .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}`.
6. Update `templates/deployment.yaml` to conditionally add `--namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}` container arg when `rbac.namespaceScoped: true` (follow existing `--leader-elect` conditional pattern).
7. Add multi-tenant deployment documentation describing when to use namespace-scoped mode, webhook limitation caveat (recommend `webhook.enabled: false`), and CRD installation remains cluster-admin concern.

Verification: `helm template` with defaults produces ClusterRole (no Role); with `rbac.namespaceScoped=true` produces Role (no ClusterRole) and `--namespace` flag. `helm lint` passes both configs. Bootstrap tests verify flag populates cache options.

**Rationale:** ClusterRole grants operator permissions across all namespaces, violating least-privilege in multi-tenant deployments. A compromised operator with ClusterRole can access resources in every namespace. Namespace-scoped RBAC limits blast radius to a single namespace, enabling secure multi-tenant patterns where each tenant gets an isolated operator instance.

**Affected Areas:**
- internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrole.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/role.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/rolebinding.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/deployment.yaml
- docs/
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Feature ID:** CC-0043

---

## Open Questions

All questions from the previous round have been answered. No remaining ambiguities.

---

## Summary

Add a Helm values toggle (`rbac.namespaceScoped`) that switches the operator's RBAC from a ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding to a namespace-scoped Role/RoleBinding. Complement this with a `--namespace` flag in the shared `bootstrap` package that restricts the controller-runtime manager's cache to a single namespace, ensuring the operator only watches and reconciles resources in its own namespace. The existing cluster-wide mode remains the default with no behavioral change.

---

## Scope

**Included:**
- Helm values toggle `rbac.namespaceScoped` (default `false`) to select Role vs ClusterRole
- New `templates/role.yaml` and `templates/rolebinding.yaml`, conditionally rendered when `rbac.namespaceScoped: true` — rules mirror the 12 apiGroup blocks in the existing `clusterrole.yaml` (182 lines)
- Conditional guard on existing `templates/clusterrole.yaml` and `templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml` (`{{- if not .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}`)
- `--namespace` string flag in `internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go` (alongside existing `--metrics-bind-address`, `--health-probe-bind-address`, `--leader-elect`, `--sync-period` flags at lines 66–84); when set, configures `cache.Options{DefaultNamespaces: map[string]cache.Config{namespace: {}}}` at line 91
- `templates/deployment.yaml` updated to conditionally pass `--namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}` as a container arg when `rbac.namespaceScoped: true` (following the existing pattern used for `--leader-elect`)
- Documentation describing the multi-tenant deployment pattern, including the webhook limitation caveat
- No regression in cluster-wide mode — default behavior unchanged

**Excluded:**
- Webhook namespace isolation — webhooks (`webhook-configuration.yaml`) are cluster-scoped Kubernetes resources; recommend disabling them (`webhook.enabled: false`) in namespace-scoped deployments and document this limitation
- Multi-namespace watching — single namespace or all; `cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces` supports a map but multi-namespace adds configuration complexity without a stated requirement (YAGNI)
- RBAC permission changes or tightening — the Role mirrors ClusterRole rules exactly, just scoped to namespace
- CRD changes — CRDs are always cluster-scoped; installation remains a cluster-admin concern
- Automated migration from ClusterRole to Role for existing deployments
- Changes to the c5c3-operator Helm chart — it has no Helm chart yet; it gains the `--namespace` flag for free via bootstrap but chart templates are out of scope
- Updates to the review pattern (`sync-rbac-markers-with-helm-clusterrole-templates.md`) — implementation detail for the planning phase

---

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph Helm["Helm Chart Rendering"]
        Values["values.yaml\nrbac.namespaceScoped"]
        Values -->|"false (default)"| CR["clusterrole.yaml +\nclusterrolebinding.yaml"]
        Values -->|"true"| R["role.yaml +\nrolebinding.yaml"]
        Values -->|"true"| DepNS["deployment.yaml\n+ --namespace flag"]
        Values -->|"false"| DepAll["deployment.yaml\nno --namespace flag"]
    end

    subgraph Runtime["Operator Runtime - bootstrap.Run"]
        Boot["bootstrap/manager.go\nparses --namespace flag"]
        Boot -->|"flag unset"| CacheAll["cache.Options\nSyncPeriod only"]
        Boot -->|"flag set"| CacheNS["cache.Options\nDefaultNamespaces + SyncPeriod"]
        CacheAll --> Reconciler["KeystoneReconciler"]
        CacheNS --> Reconciler
    end
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph ClusterWide["Cluster-Wide Mode - default"]
        SA1["ServiceAccount\nns: operator"] --> CRB["ClusterRoleBinding"]
        CRB --> CRole["ClusterRole"]
        CRole --> NS1["namespace-a"]
        CRole --> NS2["namespace-b"]
        CRole --> NS3["namespace-c"]
    end

    subgraph NamespaceScoped["Namespace-Scoped Mode"]
        SA2["ServiceAccount\nns: tenant-a"] --> RB["RoleBinding\nns: tenant-a"]
        RB --> Role["Role\nns: tenant-a"]
        Role --> NSA["tenant-a only"]
    end
```

---

## Key Components

- **`values.yaml`** (modified): New `rbac.namespaceScoped: false` toggle with documentation comments explaining the two modes
- **`templates/role.yaml`** (new): Namespace-scoped Role with identical 12 apiGroup rule blocks from `clusterrole.yaml`, rendered only when `rbac.namespaceScoped: true`
- **`templates/rolebinding.yaml`** (new): RoleBinding connecting ServiceAccount to Role, rendered only when `rbac.namespaceScoped: true`
- **`templates/clusterrole.yaml`** (modified): Wrapped in `{{- if not .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}` conditional
- **`templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml`** (modified): Same conditional guard
- **`templates/deployment.yaml`** (modified): Conditionally adds `--namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}` arg (follows the existing `--leader-elect` conditional pattern)
- **`internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go`** (modified): New `--namespace` string flag added alongside existing flags (lines 66–84); when set, populates `cache.Options.DefaultNamespaces` at line 91 — both keystone and c5c3 operators gain this capability via the shared package
- **Documentation** (new): Multi-tenant deployment guide describing when to use namespace-scoped mode, including the webhook limitation caveat (recommend `webhook.enabled: false` in namespace-scoped deployments)
